etymology.description: 額(額) (é, "forehead; quota; specified amount of money") + 頭(头) (tóu, "head; top; chief") = the front part of the head -> forehead
etymology.interpretation: 額(額) (é, "forehead") refers directly to the frontal part of the head where a person would commonly perceive their 'quota' of exposure or recognition, as it is often the most visible segment of the skull. 頭(头) (tóu, "head") specifies the general area being referred to. Together, they concretely denote the 'forehead' as the plate-like structure at the front of the head.

parts.english: forehead; quota; specified amount of money

examples.chinese: 他额头上有一颗痣(他額頭上有一顆痣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā étóu shàng yǒu yī kē zhì.
examples.english: He has a mole on his forehead.

examples.chinese: 她感到疲倦(她感到疲倦)，用手擦了擦额头(用手擦了擦額頭)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào píjuàn, yòng shǒu cāle cā étóu.
examples.english: She felt tired and wiped her forehead with her hand.

examples.chinese: 医生检查了他的额头(醫生檢查了他的額頭)，发现没有异常(發現沒有異常)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiǎnchá le tā de étóu, fāxiàn méiyǒu yìcháng.
examples.english: The doctor examined his forehead and found nothing abnormal.
